claim: Build software that owns end-to-end workflows, not isolated features—ownership of workflow creates system of record defensibility that prevents customer churn and competition
actionable: When designing AI products, ensure your solution owns the complete workflow from intake through outcome, not just one task; this creates stickiness and defensibility beyond AI feature differentiation
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Proprietary data moats compound over time—systems that generate proprietary data unavailable to labs (like case outcomes) improve their models faster than competitors using public data
actionable: Architect your AI product to generate proprietary data during normal operation; this creates a walled garden where the more you serve customers, the better your models become relative to open-source alternatives
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Greenfield vs brownfield: entering new customer segments is harder but higher-margin than displacing incumbents—new companies should target greenfield opportunities where customers have no existing tool rather than trying to displace entrenched competitors
actionable: For AI startups, identify greenfield markets (new companies, new workflows, or inflection points where existing tools fail) rather than building AI-improved versions of category leaders—greenfield has better unit economics and lower churn risk
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Best companies have hostages, not customers—companies with high switching costs and lock-in (systems of record) are more valuable long-term than companies with satisfied but easily-replaceable users
actionable: Design your product to make switching extremely difficult: own critical workflows, store irreplaceable data, and integrate deeply into customer operations; avoid being a replaceable widget that undercuts on price
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: AI enables new value propositions by changing cost-benefit ratios—software can replace labor where value > cost, but hiring humans makes no sense if cost > value; AI lowers cost so previously uneconomical workflows become profitable
actionable: When evaluating AI use cases, identify workflows where human labor exists but is expensive or undesirable (24/7 coverage, high employee turnover, regulatory complexity); optimize for value/cost ratio, not just cost reduction
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Data moats must be built via exclusivity, not just proprietary collection—lock up access to data sources (medical journals, legal records, historical data) through licensing deals or aggregation to prevent labs from training on them
actionable: Negotiate exclusive data licensing agreements with authoritative sources; if exclusive deals aren't possible, focus on aggregating and curating data that labs won't bother to collect themselves
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Model aggregation wins over single-model lock-in for end-user products—Kayak-like aggregators that search across multiple models are more valuable than products locked to a single model because models have specialized strengths
actionable: For consumer and enterprise AI tools, build against multiple model APIs rather than single-sourcing from one lab; this prevents vendor lock-in, lets you use best-in-class models for each task, and enables model-switching as landscape evolves
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Voice/audio agents enable new business models through workflow capture—voice agents can gather customer data, collect evidence, and execute multi-step processes while building proprietary datasets that improve future interactions
actionable: If deploying voice agents in customer-facing workflows, design them to gather and structure data from interactions; use this data to train specialized models and refine downstream decision-making (intake, case evaluation, prioritization)
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Expand addressable market by lowering cost thresholds—if humans charge $47k/year for a job, software charging $20k/year unlocks new customers; the total market grows when you serve previously uneconomical use cases
actionable: Price AI-native solutions 30-50% below manual labor for the same outcome; this expands the addressable market by serving customers who couldn't afford the manual labor and increases total TAM
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Contingency-based pricing aligns AI benefits with customer outcomes—when lawyers only get paid on case results, they'll invest in AI that increases win rates or case value, creating natural demand and defensibility
actionable: For labor-replacement AI, target customer segments with outcome-based compensation (contingency law, performance bonuses, commission-based roles); they'll adopt AI eagerly because it directly increases their earnings
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Incumbent software companies will struggle to transition from per-seat pricing to outcome-based pricing—they built businesses around seat-based licensing, making it hard to charge for AI-driven efficiency gains without eroding legacy revenue
actionable: If competing against incumbents in support/ERP/payroll, offer outcome-based pricing (cost per resolved ticket, cost per successful hire, etc.) rather than per-seat; this is harder for them to adopt and creates defensibility
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Labor replacement software should measure success on revenue lift, not cost savings—customers respond better to offers that promise 50% more revenue than promises to save 30% costs because revenue is less politically contentious and hits the top line
actionable: When selling AI agents replacing labor, lead with revenue/throughput improvements (50% more collections, 5x more productivity) rather than cost reduction; frame it as enabling business growth, not job elimination
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Regulatory compliance creates data moats—systems that monitor and implement multi-jurisdictional regulations (50 states, 21 languages) create defensibility because compliance updates are continuous and custom implementations are hard to replicate
actionable: For labor-replacement agents, include multi-jurisdictional compliance (state/county law monitoring, language variants, regulatory updates); this prevents competitors from easily copying your agent because they'd need custom compliance infrastructure
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Free public data can create proprietary value if aggregated, digitized, or time-series analyzed—historical data (past subscriber counts, old domain ownership, museum manuals) is free but proprietary if you're the only one who collected and curated it over time
actionable: Look for free public data sources (county records, historical web archives, eBay listings) that few have bothered to aggregate; digitize and time-series them to create proprietary datasets that have value precisely because they're rare
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Sell finished products, not raw data—curated data wrapped in AI becomes 10-100x more valuable than raw data subscriptions because customers want answers, not raw vegetables to process themselves
actionable: Don't sell raw data APIs or data subscriptions; build finished AI products that consume proprietary data and deliver actionable outputs (memos, risk scores, recommendations); this justifies 10x higher pricing
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Acquisition as distribution strategy for AI roll-ups—buy an incumbent with declining revenue but strong customers, integrate AI to improve outcomes, then sell to 10x more customers without buying more companies
actionable: For AI companies targeting national markets, acquire one declining incumbent player with blue-chip customers as your distribution anchor, then scale the customer base with AI improvements rather than acquiring dozens of smaller players
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models

claim: Vibe coding enables rapid software development but increases competitive speed—anyone can quickly build software now, making moats and switching costs more critical than ever before to avoid feature-level competition
actionable: Don't compete on features alone—use AI coding tools to move fast, but ensure your product has defensible moats (workflow lock-in, data advantages, regulatory compliance) because competitors can also code quickly
source_title: The AI Opportunity that goes beyond Models
